signals.title: EUR/USD — US-IRAN PEACE DEAL RISK
signals.text: Reports of a possible interim peace deal between the US and Iran create downside risk for the USD safe-haven bid, potentially lifting EUR/USD from its 1.1573 Friday close. This geopolitical de-escalation would likely compress the USD's risk premium, though the +167bp OIS carry advantage and EUR COT net short of -49.3k (37.4% long) remain structural headwinds. The next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hike priced) on 23 July is the primary catalyst for resolving the current policy divergence.
evidence: Headline: US-Iran peace deal under assessment
OIS carry +167bp in favor of USD

signals.title: USD/JPY — BOJ HIKE BIAS VS INTERVENTION RISK
signals.text: JPY COT net short of -115k (29.2% long) creates significant short-squeeze risk as USD/JPY holds near 160.19 amid heightened intervention warnings. While the +287bp OIS carry advantage heavily favors the USD, the JPY Economic Surprise Index (net +16) suggests domestic data outperformance is building a case for policy normalization. The BoJ meeting on 16 June is the catalyst for determining if a hawkish shift will trigger a liquidation of crowded JPY shorts.
evidence: Scotiabank reports intervention concerns at elevated levels
BoJ meeting 16 June with hike bias

signals.title: USD/CAD — OIL WEAKNESS VS EXTREME RETAIL SHORTING
signals.text: WTI Oil's 3.23% Friday slide to $84.9 pressures the CAD, but an extreme 92% retail short position in USD/CAD suggests a contrarian exhaustion risk near the 1.4000 handle. CAD COT net short of -60.6k reinforces the bearish fundamental bias, even as the pair enters overbought territory on technical indicators. The next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) is the catalyst for confirming if the current hold bias can withstand softening energy prices.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA POLICY DIVERGENCE
signals.text: AUD COT net long of +38.1k (68.8% long) faces a test as the pair closed Friday at 0.7049 ahead of a heavy central bank calendar. The -75bp OIS carry advantage for AUD and a mildly restrictive real rate of +0.17% provide fundamental support, though recent data misses (CESI net -9) suggest the RBA may struggle to maintain its hawkish relative stance. The RBA meeting on 16 June is the catalyst for validating the current long positioning.
evidence: AUD OIS carry advantage +75bp vs USD
